meta.update_trigger: FAA Part 108 finalization (expected mid-2026) — full revision required when rule drops

overview.what_is_beyond: BEYOND authorization allows DFR drones to fly Beyond Visual Line of Sight (BVLOS) — meaning the remote pilot cannot see the drone with unaided vision. Required for all true DFR programs.
overview.why_it_matters: Without BEYOND authorization, a visual observer must be stationed to watch the drone — typically on a rooftop. This dual-staffing requirement made DFR cost-prohibitive. BEYOND removes that requirement.
overview.current_state: As of May 2025, FAA overhauled the waiver process. Average approval time dropped from 11+ months to under 1 week. Some approvals in under 2 hours. 214+ approvals as of June 2025.
overview.part_108_note: FAA Part 108 (expected mid-2026) will replace case-by-case BEYOND waivers with a standardized national BVLOS framework. This guide will require full revision when Part 108 is finalized.

pathway_options.name: Part 107 BEYOND Waiver
pathway_options.best_for: Most DFR programs — patrol, crime response, SAR
pros: Fastest current pathway under new process (avg under 1 week)
Most DFR programs use this route
No annual renewal — waiver is ongoing once approved
cons: Geographic limitations tied to waiver terms
pathway_options.timeline_current: Average under 1 week; some approvals under 2 hours (as of May 2025 process change)

pathway_options.name: Certificate of Authorization (COA)
pathway_options.best_for: Persistent/ongoing operations, public aircraft operations, agencies wanting maximum flexibility
pros: Can cover larger geographic areas than Part 107 waivers
Public aircraft operations have additional flexibility
cons: Longer process than current Part 107 waiver pathway
Annual renewal required for some COA types
pathway_options.timeline_current: Varies — typically longer than Part 107 waiver

pathway_options.name: Combined Part 107 + COA
pathway_options.notes: Many active DFR programs use both. Part 107 waiver for rapid initial deployment; COA for persistent/expanded operations. Not mutually exclusive.

items.item: Platform has obstacle detection and avoidance capability
items.notes: FAA evaluates platform safety features when deciding BVLOS authorization

items.item: Platform has reliable BVLOS flight data from prior operations
items.notes: Ask manufacturer for documented BVLOS flight hours — strengthens safety case
New May 2025 process requires parachute as safety mitigation for many DFR ops
FAA safety requirement under new 2025 process
Required for automated BVLOS without visual observer. Echodyne radar integration is one pathway.

items.item: All remote pilots hold FAA Part 107 Remote Pilot Certificate

items.notes: Flying Lion, SkyfireAI offer DFR-specific BVLOS training programs
Must demonstrate operational safety procedures in waiver application

items.item: Lost link / lost communication procedures documented

items.notes: Define geographic area of DFR operations — tied to waiver approval
Operations near airports require additional coordination or altitude restrictions

items.item: LAANC coverage assessed for operational area
items.notes: LAANC automates airspace authorization below certain altitudes in controlled airspace

items.item: Local tower / TRACON coordination completed (if applicable)
General aviation density in operational area documented
items.notes: Safety case must address collision risk with GA aircraft
Specific Operations Risk Assessment — FAA does not require SORA but well-documented risk assessment strengthens application

items.item: Collision risk analysis with manned aviation documented
items.notes: Core of the safety case — show minimal risk to general aviation
Population density, people at risk if drone fails over operational area

items.item: Mitigation measures documented (parachute, DAA, altitude limits)
Select correct operation type (BVLOS / over people / night)

items.notes: 58 of 78 pending applications as of June 2025 were pending due to incomplete/illegible submissions — most common failure mode
FAA may request additional information — respond promptly or application stalls

question_1.question: Is your agency a public aircraft operation (law enforcement, fire, government)?
question_1.yes: Consider COA pathway — public aircraft have additional flexibilities

question_2.question: Do you need operations across a large geographic area (county-wide or larger)?
question_2.yes: COA may provide broader geographic coverage — evaluate both
question_2.no: Part 107 waiver is typically faster and sufficient for city/town-scale DFR

question_3.question: Do you need to begin operations quickly (within weeks)?
question_3.yes: Part 107 waiver — current avg approval under 1 week
question_3.no: Either pathway works — evaluate based on operational needs

coa_vs_part107_decision_tree.recommendation: Most DFR programs start with Part 107 waiver for speed, then add COA for expanded persistent operations. Using both is the most flexible approach.

common_failure_modes.notes: 58 of 78 pending as of June 2025 were incomplete/illegible. Review all fields before submission.
New 2025 process expects parachute as standard mitigation. Applications without it may require additional review.

common_failure_modes.failure: Insufficient safety case for collision risk
common_failure_modes.notes: Must show minimal risk to GA aircraft. Include local GA density data, altitude restrictions, DAA measures.

common_failure_modes.failure: Geographic area too large or undefined
common_failure_modes.notes: Tightly define the operational area. Vague or county-wide requests without strong safety case receive more scrutiny.
Application will be denied for federally-funded programs. Ensure NDAA compliance before applying.

service_providers.name: Flying Lion
service_providers.experience: 55,000+ DFR missions, 35,000+ BVLOS — most experienced DFR operator in the US

part_108_monitoring.what_it_is: FAA rulemaking that will replace case-by-case BVLOS waivers with a standardized national framework
part_108_monitoring.expected: Mid-2026 (based on FAA rulemaking calendar as of April 2026)
part_108_monitoring.impact: Will dramatically simplify BVLOS authorization for compliant operators — potentially eliminating the need for individual waivers for standard DFR operations
part_108_monitoring.action: Monitor Federal Register for NPRM and Final Rule. Update this guide immediately when rule drops. Notify all DFR report subscribers.
